title: Database Connector
content: The Database connector lets a Boomi process read from and write to relational databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, and SQL Server. It runs on an Atom and uses a JDBC driver to open connections to the target database.
content_html:
Database Connector Overview

The Database connector lets a Boomi process read from and write to relational databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, and SQL Server. It runs on an Atom and uses a JDBC driver to open connections to the target database.

title: Database Connector
content: Configure the database connection with the host name, port, database name, user name, and password. The connection can also reference a JDBC URL directly when you need driver-specific options. Store credentials in the connection component, not the operation.
content_html:
Connection Settings

Configure the database connection with the host name, port, database name, user name, and password. The connection can also reference a JDBC URL directly when you need driver-specific options. Store credentials in the connection component, not the operation.

title: Database Connector
content: The Database connector supports Get, Send, and Update operations. A Get operation runs a SELECT statement and returns rows as documents. A Send operation runs INSERT or stored procedures. Update runs UPDATE or DELETE statements against the target table.
content_html:
Database Operations

The Database connector supports Get, Send, and Update operations. A Get operation runs a SELECT statement and returns rows as documents. A Send operation runs INSERT or stored procedures. Update runs UPDATE or DELETE statements against the target table.

title: Database Connector
content: Enable connection pooling on the database connection to reuse open JDBC connections across process executions. Set the maximum pool size and the idle timeout so the Atom does not exhaust the database server connection limit under load.
content_html:
Connection Pooling

Enable connection pooling on the database connection to reuse open JDBC connections across process executions. Set the maximum pool size and the idle timeout so the Atom does not exhaust the database server connection limit under load.

title: Database Connector
content: Common database connection errors include invalid credentials, missing JDBC drivers on the Atom, and network firewall rules blocking the database port. Check the Atom container logs for the full JDBC exception stack trace when a connection test fails.
content_html:
Troubleshooting Database Connections

Common database connection errors include invalid credentials, missing JDBC drivers on the Atom, and network firewall rules blocking the database port. Check the Atom container logs for the full JDBC exception stack trace when a connection test fails.

title: EDI Trading Partners
content: A trading partner component represents an external business partner that you exchange EDI documents with. It bundles the communication method, the document standard, and the partner-specific envelope settings into one reusable component.
content_html:
Trading Partner Overview

A trading partner component represents an external business partner that you exchange EDI documents with. It bundles the communication method, the document standard, and the partner-specific envelope settings into one reusable component.

title: EDI Trading Partners
content: Create a trading partner by choosing the standard (X12, EDIFACT, HL7, and others), then configuring the identifier qualifiers and the classification as either My Company or Trading Partner. Each partner needs at least one communication channel.
content_html:
Creating a Trading Partner

Create a trading partner by choosing the standard (X12, EDIFACT, HL7, and others), then configuring the identifier qualifiers and the classification as either My Company or Trading Partner. Each partner needs at least one communication channel.

title: EDI Trading Partners
content: Trading partners exchange documents over AS2, SFTP, FTP, disk, or HTTP. AS2 is the most common channel for X12 in North America because it provides signed, encrypted, non-repudiated delivery with synchronous or asynchronous MDN receipts.
content_html:
Communication Channels

Trading partners exchange documents over AS2, SFTP, FTP, disk, or HTTP. AS2 is the most common channel for X12 in North America because it provides signed, encrypted, non-repudiated delivery with synchronous or asynchronous MDN receipts.

title: EDI Trading Partners
content: The X12 standard is widely used in North America while EDIFACT is common internationally. Each standard defines envelopes, functional groups, and transaction sets. Boomi validates inbound documents against the standard before the process runs.
content_html:
Document Standards

The X12 standard is widely used in North America while EDIFACT is common internationally. Each standard defines envelopes, functional groups, and transaction sets. Boomi validates inbound documents against the standard before the process runs.

title: EDI Trading Partners
content: When EDI documents fail, check the envelope identifiers, the control numbers, and the acknowledgement (997 or CONTRL) status. A mismatch between the partner's expected qualifier and the configured qualifier is the most frequent cause of rejected interchanges.
content_html:
Trading Partner Troubleshooting

When EDI documents fail, check the envelope identifiers, the control numbers, and the acknowledgement (997 or CONTRL) status. A mismatch between the partner's expected qualifier and the configured qualifier is the most frequent cause of rejected interchanges.

title: Map Component
content: The Map component defines how source profile fields are transformed into destination profile fields. A map connects a source profile to a destination profile and can apply functions, default values, and cross-reference lookups as documents pass through the process.
content_html:
Map Component Overview

The Map component defines how source profile fields are transformed into destination profile fields. A map connects a source profile to a destination profile and can apply functions, default values, and cross-reference lookups as documents pass through the process.

title: Map Component
content: Drag a function onto the map canvas and wire source fields into its inputs and its outputs into destination fields. Chain functions to build multi-step transformations such as string concatenation, date formatting, or numeric rounding before the value reaches the destination profile.
content_html:
Adding Function Steps

Drag a function onto the map canvas and wire source fields into its inputs and its outputs into destination fields. Chain functions to build multi-step transformations such as string concatenation, date formatting, or numeric rounding before the value reaches the destination profile.

title: Map Component
content: Set a default value on a destination field so the map emits a value even when the source field is empty. Document caches let a map look up values captured earlier in the process without re-querying the source system for every document.
content_html:
Default Values and Caching

Set a default value on a destination field so the map emits a value even when the source field is empty. Document caches let a map look up values captured earlier in the process without re-querying the source system for every document.

title: Map Component
content: Expose a map for environment-specific overrides by enabling map extensions. An operator can then remap fields or adjust default values per environment at deployment time without editing the underlying component in the build tab.
content_html:
Map Extensions

Expose a map for environment-specific overrides by enabling map extensions. An operator can then remap fields or adjust default values per environment at deployment time without editing the underlying component in the build tab.
